title: Hydrogen
content:
As global demand for clean energy solutions intensifies, we stand at the forefront of advising clients on hydrogen fuel innovation and development. Our tailored solutions offer a strategic advantage in addressing opportunities and challenges in the evolving clean hydrogen landscape.
Hydrogen fuel development is one of the key technologies shaping the energy transition. We have deep knowledge of all aspects of hydrogen project development, spanning the hydrogen colour spectrum from green and blue hydrogen generation to other hydrogen generation and associated carbon capture, utilization and storage (CCUS) projects.
Our Strategic Advisors have unique relationships with regulators, key officials and policymakers, both provincially and federally, which allow us to be among the first to learn of the evolving policies driving hydrogen project development. Leveraging this insight and our hydrogen and renewable energy expertise, together with our robust experience in licensing, mergers & acquisitions, financing, development and regulator matters, we can provide you with a strategic advantage in guiding you through new opportunities and challenges as the hydrogen market continues to grow.
- Confidential Client in connection with a transaction involving a significant investment in an international company that owns hydrogen projects in Canada.
- Confidential Client in connection with a joint venture for the development and construction of a significant green hydrogen plant in Québec.
- Confidential Client in connection with the development, ownership and operation of a carbon sequestration hub in Alberta associated with a proposed hydrogen power project.
- Shell Canada in connection with its acquisition of a 40% interest in the Varennes Carbon Recycling Plant, a CAD $875 million commercial-scale facility producing low-carbon fuels and renewable products from non-recyclable waste.
- Syndicate of Lenders (CAD $1.5 billion) in connection with the financing of Alberta’s new Cascade Power Project.
- Counsel to the lenders in Inter Pipeline Ltd.’s divestiture of Heartland Petrochemical Complex’s Central Utility Block to Fengate Capital Management, a $3.5 billion complex with integrated propane dehydrogenation and polypropylene plants to convert locally sourced, low-cost propane into polypropylene pellets.
